enonce.texte: Match the healthcare terms on the left with their correct definitions on the right. Write the letter of the correct definition next to each number.

questions.texte: Match each term to its definition.

sousQuestions.reponse: c. A place where sick or injured people receive medical treatment
e. A substance that protects people from a disease
a. A medical professional who examines patients and prescribes treatment
b. Medical care given to a patient for an illness or injury
d. A drug used to cure or prevent disease

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences below with the correct healthcare vocabulary from the box. Each word can be used only once.

Word box: hospital, clinic, doctor, nurse, vaccine, treatment, medicine, cure, patient, prescription

questions.texte: The __________ examined the child and diagnosed malaria.
After the accident, he was rushed to the nearest __________.
The __________ gave the patient an injection to reduce the fever.
Scientists are working hard to find a __________ for HIV/AIDS.
You need a doctor's __________ to buy this antibiotic.
The COVID-19 __________ has saved millions of lives worldwide.
She went to the local __________ because she had a high fever.
The __________ stayed in bed for three days to recover from malaria.
The doctor prescribed some __________ for the infection.
Early diagnosis and proper __________ can prevent complications.

enonce.texte: Complete the dialogue between a doctor and a patient using appropriate healthcare vocabulary. Fill in each blank with a suitable word or phrase.

questions.texte: Doctor: Good morning. What seems to be the __________?
Patient: I have a high __________ and a severe headache.
Doctor: How long have you had these __________?
Patient: For about three days. I also feel very weak and tired.
Doctor: I will __________ you to find out what is wrong.
Doctor: You have malaria. I will write you a __________ for medication.
Doctor: Take this __________ twice a day after meals.
Patient: Thank you, doctor. How long will the __________ take?
Doctor: You should feel better within five days. Get plenty of rest.
Patient: I will. Should I come back for a __________?

intitule: Real-Life Problem Solving: Choosing Healthcare Services

enonce.texte: Read each scenario carefully. Then answer the questions using complete sentences and appropriate healthcare vocabulary. Justify your choices.

questions.texte: Scenario 1: Your 5-year-old sister has had a high fever (39.5°C) for two days, is coughing continuously, and seems very weak. Your mother is at work and cannot be reached. What should you do? Justify your answer using at least three healthcare terms.
questions.reponse: I should take my sister to the nearest hospital or clinic immediately. A doctor or nurse will examine her, diagnose the problem, and provide appropriate treatment. The symptoms (high fever, cough, weakness) could indicate a serious illness like malaria or pneumonia that requires prompt medical attention.

questions.texte: Scenario 2: Your grandfather has diabetes. He forgot to take his medicine this morning and now feels dizzy and confused. What is the most appropriate action? Explain why using healthcare vocabulary.
questions.reponse: I should help him take his prescribed medicine immediately if he is conscious and able to swallow. Then I should monitor his symptoms. If he does not improve quickly, I should call a doctor or take him to the nearest clinic or hospital. Diabetes requires regular treatment, and missing medication can lead to serious health complications.

questions.texte: Scenario 3: A new vaccine against a dangerous disease is available at the local health center, but some people in your community are afraid to take it because of false rumors. As a responsible citizen, how would you convince them to get vaccinated? Use at least four healthcare terms in your answer.
questions.reponse: I would explain that the vaccine is safe and effective. It protects individuals from the disease and helps create community immunity. Doctors and nurses recommend vaccination as the best form of prevention. Without the vaccine, people are at risk of infection, which could lead to severe symptoms, expensive treatment, or even death. I would encourage them to visit the health center and speak with a medical professional who can answer their questions.

intitule: Sentence Transformation: Active to Passive (Healthcare Context)

enonce.texte: Transform the following active sentences into passive voice. The topic is healthcare. Write the complete passive sentence.

questions.texte: The doctor prescribes antibiotics for bacterial infections.
questions.reponse: Antibiotics are prescribed by the doctor for bacterial infections.

questions.texte: The nurse will administer the vaccine tomorrow.
questions.reponse: The vaccine will be administered by the nurse tomorrow.

questions.texte: Scientists have developed a new treatment for malaria.
questions.reponse: A new treatment for malaria has been developed by scientists.

questions.texte: The pharmacist gave me the medicine this morning.
questions.reponse: The medicine was given to me by the pharmacist this morning.

questions.texte: They are building a new hospital in our district.
questions.reponse: A new hospital is being built in our district.

enonce.texte: Each sentence below contains ONE error related to healthcare vocabulary, grammar, or word form. Identify the error, correct it, and rewrite the entire sentence correctly.

questions.texte: The patient was given a medical treatment for his injure.
questions.reponse: The patient was given medical treatment for his injury. (Error: 'injure' should be the noun 'injury')

questions.texte: The nurse take the patient's temperature every four hours.
questions.reponse: The nurse takes the patient's temperature every four hours. (Error: subject-verb agreement - 'nurse' is singular, so 'takes')

questions.texte: If you don't feel well, you should went to the clinic immediately.
questions.reponse: If you don't feel well, you should go to the clinic immediately. (Error: after modal 'should', use base verb 'go' not past tense 'went')

questions.texte: The medicament was very effective against the virus.
questions.reponse: The medicine was very effective against the virus. (Error: 'medicament' is not common in English; use 'medicine' or 'medication')

questions.texte: She has been suffering from headache since three days.
questions.reponse: She has been suffering from headaches for three days. (Error: with present perfect continuous, use 'for' + duration, not 'since')

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are a health officer in your community. Write a short public health announcement (150-200 words) to educate people about preventing the spread of a common disease (malaria, cholera, or COVID-19). Your announcement must:

  • Identify the disease and its main symptoms
  • Explain preventive measures
  • Encourage people to seek treatment at healthcare facilities
  • Use at least 8 different healthcare vocabulary words from Lesson 31
  • Use appropriate modal verbs (should, must, can) to give advice and obligation

questions.texte: Write your public health announcement in the space below.
questions.reponse:
Sample answer:

PUBLIC HEALTH ANNOUNCEMENT: PREVENTING MALARIA

Dear community members,

Malaria remains a dangerous disease in our area. The main symptoms include high fever, severe headache, chills, and body weakness. If you experience these symptoms, you must visit the nearest hospital or clinic immediately for proper diagnosis.

A doctor or nurse will examine you and provide appropriate treatment. Early treatment can save your life. You should take prescribed medicine exactly as directed.

To prevent malaria, you must sleep under an insecticide-treated bed net every night. You should eliminate standing water around your home where mosquitoes breed. You can also use mosquito repellent and wear long sleeves at dusk.

Pregnant women and young children should receive preventive vaccines and regular check-ups. If you have a fever, do not self-medicate. Go to a health center for a proper consultation.

Remember: prevention is better than cure. Protect yourself and your family. Visit our local clinic for free malaria testing this week!

Your health is our priority.

- The District Health Office
